# Development Workflow

<!-- Generated by skill-conductor setup. Customize for your project. -->

## Workflow: Context → Spec → Plan → Implement → Review → Publish

### Phase 1: Context (setup — once per project)
- Define project goals and user needs
- Establish writing guidelines
- Configure reference sources

### Phase 2: Spec (new)
- Describe the change in detail
- Define trigger words
- List files to create/modify
- Set acceptance criteria

### Phase 3: Plan (new)
- Break spec into phases
- Create checkbox task list
- Define verification checkpoints

### Phase 4: Implement
- Follow plan.md sequentially
- Update checklist as tasks complete
- Pause at phase boundaries for user verification

### Phase 5: Review
- Run 7-step review pipeline
- Fix any FAIL items before proceeding
- Apply WARN suggestions as needed

### Phase 6: Publish (optional)
- Version bump
- Update CHANGELOG
- Git commit + tag + push
- GitHub release

## Commit Convention
```
<type>(<scope>): <description>

Types: feat, fix, docs, refactor, chore
Scope: skill name or "core", "docs", "config"
```

## Version Convention
Follow Semantic Versioning (semver.org):
- MAJOR: Breaking changes to skill behavior
- MINOR: New skills or new features
- FIX: Bug fixes, description improvements
